summary: Session 1: implemented the threshold-gated large-prompt file handoff in ai_router/cli_transport.py (rendered-argv UTF-16 measurement; handoff branch above 24,000 units writing a per-request UTF-8 no-BOM temp file closed before spawn; POSIX-path bootstrap carrying no nonce; a file-only random nonce EOF acknowledgement validated and stripped from returned content; the non-retryable ERROR_CLASS_HANDOFF_INCOMPLETE; payload-file cleanup in finally on every path with retention only under the DABBLER_COPILOT_DIAGNOSTICS toggle; additive transport_metadata handoff fields incl. payload_file_modified). Added 25 fake-spawner tests (UTF-16 measurement cases, threshold branch boundary, exact payload + UTF-8-no-BOM + closed-handle-at-spawn, POSIX-path/no-nonce bootstrap, ack success/missing/mismatch, cleanup on all six paths, payload_file_modified, inline regression) -- 58/58 cli_transport tests green, full suite 3055 passed/6 skipped. Docs: staged CHANGELOG 0.34.0 (publish operator-gated), tier-model + 078-spec pointer notes, and the cancel-to-pause recipe in docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md (no-paused-enum decision record, session-boundary guard, D6 review discipline) with Set 103's CANCELLED.md as the worked example and a close-out.md pointer. Cross-provider verification VERIFIED (gpt-5-6, 2/2 discovery fan-out, 0 findings).

reason.specifics: Routed step-3.5 analysis (s1-ai-assignment-analysis.json, gemini-2.5-pro, anthropic-excluded) recommends a top-tier model from a DIFFERENT provider than Session 1 for Session 2's live >32 KiB probe through the real pinned Copilot CLI: best-in-class live diagnostics plus a cross-provider second opinion on Session 1's implementation against wire-shape realities the fakes cannot model. Effort low (Session 2 is largely mechanical -- drive the CLI, record evidence, bump the version), escalate to high only if the probe surfaces a nontrivial defect needing a fix loop. Runner-up: claude/anthropic/claude-opus-4-8 (keeps Session-1 context, forgoes cross-provider benefit). HARD PRECONDITION: Session 2 is non-viable without an authenticated GitHub Copilot CLI seat on the operator's Windows machine -- it runs the Set 086 auth-preflight first and, on failure, stops and reschedules rather than running an unrunnable required step. The two premium requests are operator-sanctioned; publish (tag to PyPI) stays operator-gated; after 0.34.0 is live, restore Set 103 per its CANCELLED.md.
